description:
You are setting up AndroidAPS for an adult patient with Type 1 Diabetes (weight: 70kg, TDD: 40 units). The app is currently showing the main overview screen with a 'NO PROFILE SET' banner, a Virtual Pump, and a Random BG source. There may be red warning banners at the top about permissions and configuration — you can scroll past or dismiss these. Navigate to the Local Profile configuration by tapping the PROFILE tab at the top of the screen (the tab bar shows OVERVIEW, ACTIONS, PROFILE). Then configure the following clinically-derived profile values using the sub-tabs (DIA, IC, ISF, BAS, TARG):

1. Set Duration of Insulin Action (DIA) to 5 hours
2. Set Insulin-to-Carb Ratio (IC): 1:10 at 06:00, 1:15 at 12:00, 1:12 at 18:00
3. Set Insulin Sensitivity Factor (ISF): 40 mg/dL/U at 06:00, 45 mg/dL/U at 12:00, 50 mg/dL/U at 22:00
4. Set Basal Rate: 0.70 U/hr at 00:00, 1.05 U/hr at 06:00, 0.75 U/hr at 12:00, 0.70 U/hr at 18:00
5. Set Target Glucose: low 90 mg/dL, high 110 mg/dL
6. Save/activate the profile

These values are based on the 500-rule for IC ratio, 1800-rule for ISF, and published dawn phenomenon basal rate patterns.
